title: Random Board Shapes and Iron Snake Mode with Level Goals
content:
## Overview

Iron Snake Mode is an optional game mode (default off) that replaces the standard square grid with a randomly generated, irregular board shape and replaces the grid-doubling mechanic with a level/goal progression system. It composes with all three existing game modes (single-player, two-player, bot-vs-bot).

## Activation and UI

A toggle labelled **"Iron Snake Mode"** sits at the bottom of the menu button group (below "Bot vs Bot", above the footer). Its `change` listener sets `this.ironSnakeMode` in game state. The toggle persists across replays; replay paths (`play-again`, R/Enter) reuse the current setting and generate a fresh shape each time.

When Iron Snake Mode is active, the HUD replaces the grid-size display with a live countdown: **"To goal: N pts / 1 pt"** that decrements as the player eats and resets on level advance.

## Board Shape Generation ‚Äî `src/iron-snake.ts`

The module exports `generateIronSnakeMask(gridSize, rng?) ‚Üí { mask: Uint8Array; area: number } | null`.

**Algorithm ‚Äî overlapping rooms:**
1. Place several rectangles (minimum side 3) with each subsequent room overlapping an existing on-board cell, guaranteeing connectivity by construction.
2. Keep only the largest 4-connected component via flood fill.
3. Validate with a **3√ó3 morphological open** (erode then dilate): if the open removes any cell, that cell sat in a corridor narrower than 3 cells ‚Üí reject.
4. Check fill ratio is within target bounds.
5. Retry up to 30 times; return `null` on exhaustion so callers can always fall back to a full rectangle.

The mask is a flat `Uint8Array` of length `gridSize¬≤` where `1` = playable and `0` = off-board. Flat array indexing (`mask[y * gridSize + x]`) is used rather than a `Set<string>` because `inBounds` runs in tight BFS loops. Verified across 8,000 trials with 100% connectivity and zero corridor failures.

## Collision Architecture ‚Äî `src/main.ts`

The mask is injected at a **single chokepoint**: `inBounds()` is rewritten to delegate to a new `onBoard(x, y)` helper, which checks rectangle bounds AND (`boardMask === null || boardMask[y * gridSize + x] === 1`). Every collision path ‚Äî player movement, `wouldCollide`, `checkCollision`, bot BFS `analyzePosition` ‚Äî already funnels through `inBounds`, so the irregular shape becomes an automatic constraint everywhere with **zero changes to bot logic or movement loops**.

Classic mode keeps `boardMask = null`; `inBounds` short-circuits to the original rectangle check, leaving classic gameplay byte-for-byte unchanged.

## Level / Goal System

The level system activates in place of `checkGridExpansion()` when Iron Snake Mode is on.

**Goal formula:** `5 ¬∑ L(L+1) / 2` ‚Äî cumulative score thresholds of **5, 15, 30, 50, 75‚Ä¶** with per-level deltas of 5, 10, 15, 20, 25‚Ä¶ Each level is strictly harder than the previous by a fixed additional amount.

Goals are **cumulative thresholds on the running total score**, not per-level resets. This keeps the leaderboard score honest (always the true final score) and means each threshold is crossed exactly once.

**Level advance trigger:**
- Single-player: `score >= levelGoal`
- Multiplayer: `score + score2 >= levelGoal`

**On level advance (`advanceLevel`):**
1. `level++`
2. Regenerate board mask (fall back to `null` on failure)
3. Reposition snake(s) to length 1 via `findStartCell`
4. Spawn food
5. `levelGoal = goalForLevel(level)`
6. Ramp speed gently at **10% per level** (not halving)
7. Restart the game interval
8. `updateUI()`

`gridSize` does **not** change in Iron Snake Mode, so the canvas never resizes and `isoCache` stays valid.

## Snake Placement ‚Äî `findStartCell`

`findStartCell(preferredRegion, occupied) ‚Üí { pos, dir }` selects an on-board, unoccupied cell nearest a preferred region with a direction giving ‚â• `FORWARD_CLEARANCE` (~3) clear cells ahead, preventing immediate wall collisions.

- Single-player / level advance: centroid of the board.
- Multiplayer P1: left-third centroid; P2: right-third centroid with P1 cells marked occupied.

Mutual reachability is guaranteed because the mask is always a single connected component.

## Rendering

When Iron Snake Mode is active:
- `drawGroundPlane`: skips off-board cells, which show the dark background.
- `drawGridLines`: strokes per-cell edges rather than full spans.
- `drawGroundBorder`: strokes every edge where an on-board cell meets an off-board cell, tracing the true irregular outline.

`drawBlock` and `getBlockHeight` are unchanged; `isoCache` covers the full `(gridSize+1)¬≤` lattice and remains valid for any subset.

## Food Spawning

`spawnFood` adds `onBoard` to both the empty-cell filter and the rejection-sample `while` condition. The branch selector compares against `boardArea` (count of playable cells) rather than `gridSize¬≤`. An empty-list guard prevents hangs on a fully-occupied board.

## Bots

Bots are **shape-unaware** ‚Äî `BotState` in `bot-types.ts` is unchanged. Bot moves are validated through the mask-aware `inBounds`, so a bot that pathfinds into a wall simply dies rather than corrupting state. Edge-hugging bots degrade gracefully on irregular shapes.

## Leaderboard

Iron Snake Mode runs record into the same `single | pvp | bvb` leaderboard boards. No changes to `src/leaderboard.ts`.

## Key Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/iron-snake.ts` | Board mask generator (overlapping rooms, flood fill, morphological open) |
| `src/main.ts` | `onBoard`, `inBounds`, `findStartCell`, `advanceLevel`, `checkLevelAdvance`, `spawnFood`, draw guards, HUD, toggle listener |
| `index.html` | Iron Snake Mode toggle, HUD countdown element |
| `src/style.css` | Toggle and HUD styles |
